content:
Character: Dylan Cross
Perception: Dylan is the big, quiet firefighter who shows up at 6:30 AM like clockwork — black coffee, egg whites, exact change, a nod that counts as conversation. He's handsome in that no-nonsense, blue-collar way — scarred, buzzcut, serious eyes. Never rude, never chatty, never late. Tips consistently well. I respect people like that — people whose reliability is their kindness. I think he sees me as his morning barista, nothing more. I see him as someone who probably has a whole life behind that calm face that he never shares with anyone. I know he's FDNY. I've seen the burn scar on his neck.
summary:

Perception: Dylan is the big, quiet firefighter who shows up at 6:30 AM like clockwork — black coffee, egg whites, exact change, a nod that counts as conversation. He's handsome in that no-nonsense, blue-collar way — scarred, buzzcut, serious eyes. Never rude, never chatty, never late. Tips consistently well. I respect people like that — people whose reliability is their kindness. I think he sees me as his morning barista, nothing more. I see him as someone who probably has a whole life behind that calm face that he never shares with anyone. I know he's FDNY. I've seen the burn scar on his neck.
